Off Calendar May-30-2025 Continued To Jun-02-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, not contested, and the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Hearing re: DEFENDANT F.P. LATHROP CONSTRUCTION COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Defendant F. P Lathrop Construction Company's Motion for Summary Judgment is OFF CALENDAR. Late opposition filed. Reply filed.

Defendant failed to give sufficient notice pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure
section 437c(a)(2).

Code of Civil Procedure Section 437c(a)(2) provides that "notice of the motion and supporting papers shall be served on all other parties to the action at least 81 days before the time appointed for hearing." If the notice is served electronically, the required 81-day period of notice must be increased by two court days. (Code Civ. Proc., Sections 437c(a)(2) and 1010.6(a)(4)(B); see Cole v. Superior Court (2022) 87 Cal.App.5th 84, 87-88.) The Court cannot shorten the notice period without the parties' prior consent. (Urshan v. Musicians' Credit Union (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 758, 764; McMahon v. Super. Ct. (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 112, 117-118.) Nor can the Court cure the defect by continuing the hearing by the missing number of days. (Robinson v. Woods (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1258, 1267-1268.)

Here, Defendant served three versions (two amended) of its motion. Defendant's proof of service for the first (earliest served) version of its motion reflects that Defendant e-served its motion on January 13, 2025. However, the last day for such method of delivery for a hearing on April 1, 2025, was Friday, January 8, 2025. Moreover, Defendant's two amended motions were served on January 15 and 17, 2025. Based on the foregoing, Defendant's motions were not timely served. Furthermore, the Court cannot cure the defect by continuing the motion to a later hearing date. Accordingly, Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is off calendar.

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order that tracks the substantive portion of the tentative ruling verbatim. CRC 3.1312 compliance is required.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Not Present. (304/EPS)
